title: Where Everything Is Dull
wikitext:
{{User:JakitoFan/graytheme}} '''Where Everything Is Dull''', also known as '''The Greyscale Park''', is an anomalous and hard-to-access level in the Backrooms. ==Description== {{Blurimage|file=Joel-filipe-pyHPJEjUYHY-unsplash.jpg|caption=An image of '''Where Everything Is Dull'''.|hovertext=Monochromatic Mystery.}}{{Blurimage|file=5535644.jpg|caption=A second image of '''Where Everything Is Dull'''.|hovertext=Greyscale Geometry.}} '''Where Everything Is Dull''' consists of a boundless maze of rooms with curved and occasionally symmetrical structures dipped entirely in shades of greyscale, composed of a mix of concrete and aluminum surfaces. Aluminum bars are visible throughout the level and stretch every which way, creating a matrix that makes the level slightly harder to navigate. These bars can seldom lead to holes in the level, revealing what appears to be a blinding white void in between the fabric of the level. The different slopes of this level each carry their own consistent gravitational force, equivalent to that of Earth's gravity. This enables the wanderer to be able to walk on all surfaces within the level regardless of if standard gravity would allow it. These forces do not interact with each other in any capacity for reasons yet to be determined. Consequently, wanderers are prevented from being tangled in two gravities or more at once and potentially getting injured. The level is unanimously described as dull, frigid, and dry upon entrance. The air is still, and no winds permeate the level. Despite its size, the level does not transmit sound well; most sounds made do not even escape the room they were made in. The total dimensions of the level are unknown and presumably infinite. The level's shape is ambiguous as it slowly alters its details when eye contact is not made on those areas, such that given enough time~5 hours on average, depending on the room's size a room's layout can be completely changed. This process is completely silent. These traits lead to the level being known for extreme volatility. Noclipping does not function whatsoever in this level. Attempting to noclip causes one to instantly return to the point where they entered the level; if the room has changed significantly it will bring one to the nearest point of ground within the room they entered in. Another notable aspect of the level is that sleeping in the level is notoriously difficult, and success consistently leads to the geometry of the level frenetically altering upon waking up, regardless of how long they slept. The level is lit through anomalous means, with no apparent light source. The level is desolate, lacking any form of supplies or furnishings, meaning wanderers must bring a large quantity of supplies if they arrive intentionally. ---- ==Depths== '''Where Everything Is Dull''' includes a multitude of sub-layers, each one with notable differences. These sublevels are only accessible through black concrete doorways. The doorways will appear to lead nowhere, but walking through will cause one to black out and awaken on the floor of the next sublayer. Each sublayer becomes more unstable, and the geometry becomes wilder and unbalanced. ====Sublayer I - Modicum==== Upon entering a black concrete doorway in '''Where Everything Is Dull''' and walking through, one will end up in '''Modicum''', officially documented as '''Sublayer I'''. '''Sublayer I''' shares a multitude of similarities with '''Where Everything Is Dull''', with the notable exception of warped geometry. Misalignments may appear in '''Sublayer I''' that cannot appear in its main level due to its structural stability. Small cracks may appear leading to the aforementioned white void. '''Sublayer I''' is also significantly less stable than its parent level, but stable compared to the later sublayers. ====Sublayer II - Partial==== Repeating the exit method from '''Where Everything Is Dull''' to '''Sublayer I''' while in '''Sublayer I''' will lead to '''Partial''', officially documented as '''Sublayer II'''. '''Sublayer II''' continues the trend established by '''Sublayer I''', with the architectural instability increasing. Cracks, damaged walls and even the rare gaping hole into the white void are not uncommon in '''Sublayer II'''. '''Sublayer II''' and beyond begin to completely prevent sleep, forcing the wanderer awake if they attempt to doze off. '''Sublayer II''''s lighting will begin to fail, a trend that continues with '''Sublayer III'''. ====Sublayer III - Major==== {{Blurimage|file=Photo-1597415884160-da3e81008820111.jpg|caption=An image of '''Sublayer III'''. Note the unusual high contrast.|hovertext=Chaotic Colorlessness}} Repeating the exit method once more in '''Sublayer II''' will lead to '''Major''', documented officially as '''Sublayer III'''. This sublayer is the first outright hazardous sublayer of '''Where Everything Is Dull'''. The architectural stability of '''Sublayer III''' begins to rapidly decline, not just becoming a fixture in the appearance of the sublayer. '''Sublayer III''''s structure has the potential to collapse in on itself, possibly crushing those underneath. No-clipping in '''Sublayer III''' leads to a random location within the sublayer without warning. '''Sublayer III''' is slowly rotating, which serves to unnerve the wanderer. Periodically in '''Sublayer III''', wanderers will describe the feeling of falling despite the fact that their feet are on solid ground. The concrete and aluminum will begin to soften in some places, creating further architectural instability. The contrast of '''Sublayer III''' will highly increase, with most shades of grey being replaced with black and white. This is a highly dangerous sublayer. ====The Breaking Point==== {{Blurimage|file=Breakingpoint.jpg|caption=An image of Sublayer IV, The Breaking Point. Photographer most likely deceased.|hovertext=For thee.}}'''The Breaking Point''', the final sublayer of '''Where Everything Is Dull''', is officially documented '''Sublayer IV'''. The structure of '''Sublayer IV''' rotates slightly faster than '''Sublayer III''', serving to unnerve the wanderers who enter. The walls and floor begin to shift regardless of if eye contact is held. The architecture will begin to actively break down, with entire square meters of concrete and aluminum falling into the white void without warning. The contrast of '''Sublayer IV''' is now entirely black and white. Lighting within this sublayer is of low luminosity, with some sections dipped in darkness. This is the first and only sublayer with an entity presence, with [[Entity 3|Smilers]] being present in the darkest depths of the level. Co-ordination is difficult to assess, with the vibration and rotation causing panic and anxiety at this stage. Wanderers report a feeling of nausea when within '''Sublayer IV'''. Unusually, wanderers will also become slightly nauseous when looking at images or videos of the sublayer. '''Sublayer IV''' is the most fatal of the sublayers of '''Where Everything Is Dull''', and exiting from this sublayer is nigh-impossible. Only 12 escapees have been recorded from '''Sublayer IV'''. ==Colonies and Outposts== [[Phenomenon 15]] affects this level, preventing wanderers from interacting. Even if [[Phenomenon 15]] had not affected the level, the lack of supplies would make colonies logistically impossible. ==Thresholds== ===Ingresses=== No consistent methods of entering this level have been discovered thus far. However, the highest frequency of entry comes from levels with little to no colors, such as [[Level 483]], [[Level 484]] and [[Level 922]]. ===Egresses=== Egresses are inconsistent as well, not dissimilar to the exits from [[Level 27]]. Each wanderer has recorded their own method of escape, and rarely do more than 5 stories from escapees match. =='''The Antivoid'''== {{Blurimage|file=White with minimal bytes.png|caption=An image of '''The Antivoid'''.}}'''The Antivoid''', also colloquially called 'The Whole'In reference to how [[The Void]] is empty, appears to be an infinitely luminous white space that expands infinitely and in a non-Euclidean manner in all directions. '''The Antivoid''' can be accessed by entering any cracks that display '''The Antivoid''' beyond them. '''The Antivoid''' appears to be in between '''Where Everything Is Dull''' and associated sublevels. Those who enter report that they hear the sound of howling winds from their entrance to their exit. Exits from '''The Antivoid''' are rare, but no recorded escapees have stayed present in '''The Antivoid''' for a period longer than one weekThis is possibly connected to the constant ''e'', as the duration is an estimated ''e'' *''e'' days.. Exits from '''The Antivoid''' always lead to monochromatic levels, but not necessarily greyscale ones.

Thank you for reading. ^^ And yes, I borrowed Egglord1's source code.
